compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can validate recipes against real open-source codebases
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Integration test suite that validates each recipe against a real open-source codebase fixture, asserting semantic correctness by running the codebase's own test suite after the transform.
**Why**: Unit tests verify recipe mechanics; integration tests against real codebases verify that recipes produce semantically correct transforms at scale (e.g., add-parameter on a codebase with 15+ callers across 8 files).
**Scope**:
- IN: Integration test harness, real open-source codebase fixtures (cloned from public repos), recipe execution against fixtures, semantic verification via fixture's own test suite, CI caching/vendoring of fixtures
- OUT: Recipe implementation (S1-S4), new recipe development

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, each recipe (add-parameter, scaffold-route, normalize-imports) is tested against at least one real open-source codebase representative of its target stack
  ← Satisfies: "Each recipe is tested against a real open-source codebase fixture"
- When this story is complete, the codebase's own test suite passes after each recipe transform, verifying semantic correctness
  ← Satisfies: "asserts semantic correctness — verified by the codebase's own test suite passing after the transform"
- When this story is complete, test fixtures are cached or vendored so CI runs don't require network access for each test
  ← Satisfies: reliable CI execution
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Each test run should use a fresh clone to isolate from the executor's git stash rollback mechanism — stash interactions with the cloned repo's git state must be avoided
- [Testing] Real codebases may have non-deterministic test elements (timestamps, generated files) — select fixtures with stable, deterministic test suites
- [Reliability] Network access required for initial clone — CI workflow should cache cloned fixtures as artifacts

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
